interfaces.name: Player
interfaces.signature: struct Player: Identifiable, Codable, Hashable { let id: UUID; var name: String; var colorIndex: Int }
interfaces.notes: colorIndex indexes into the fixed 6-hue player palette from design tokens; red reserved for trigger/false-start.

interfaces.name: GamePhase
interfaces.signature: enum GamePhase: Codable, Hashable { case setup, howToPlay, heatAssignment, arena, eliminationReveal, roundSummary, gameOver }
interfaces.notes: Single source of truth for what MatchContainerView renders; navigation must never diverge from this.

interfaces.name: Heat
interfaces.signature: struct Heat: Identifiable, Codable { let id: UUID; var playerIDs: [Player.ID]; var survivorTarget: Int; var isComplete: Bool }
interfaces.notes: Only populated when player count exceeds simultaneousCap (default 6). Default survivorTarget rounds UP (ceil(count/2)) so a small odd heat never advances zero players.

interfaces.name: EliminationReason
interfaces.signature: enum EliminationReason: String, Codable { case falseStart, slowLift }

interfaces.name: EliminationRecord
interfaces.signature: struct EliminationRecord: Identifiable, Codable { let id: UUID; let playerID: Player.ID; let reason: EliminationReason; let roundNumber: Int; let heatIndex: Int? }

interfaces.name: Match
interfaces.signature: struct Match: Codable { var id: UUID; var players: [Player]; var heats: [Heat]; var activeHeatIndex: Int?; var roundNumber: Int; var eliminationLog: [EliminationRecord]; var phase: GamePhase }
interfaces.notes: This exact struct is what MatchSnapshotStore persists on backgrounding.

interfaces.name: HeatPlanner
interfaces.signature: enum HeatPlanner { static func plan(players: [Player], simultaneousCap: Int = 6) -> [Heat] }
interfaces.notes: Pure, nonisolated function; handles exact-cap boundary, odd totals, and a heat producing only one survivor. Callable from background test threads without actor hops.

interfaces.name: GameEngine
interfaces.signature: @Observable @MainActor final class GameEngine { private(set) var match: Match?; private(set) var route: AppRoute; func startMatch(players: [Player]) throws(MatchStartError); func beginNextRound(); func applyArenaVerdict(_ verdict: ArenaVerdict); func advanceAfterSummary(); func abandonMatch(); func handleScenePhaseChange(_ phase: ScenePhase); func resumeIfAvailable() -> Bool }
interfaces.notes: Sole owner of match + navigation route. applyArenaVerdict is called synchronously from ArenaUIView's touch handlers, which already run on main — no actor-hop needed.

interfaces.name: AppRoute
interfaces.signature: enum AppRoute: Hashable { case playerSetup, matchPlay, settings }
interfaces.notes: Intentionally shallow — NavigationStack path never grows per-round; MatchContainerView owns in-match transitions internally via GamePhase.

interfaces.name: TouchSample
interfaces.signature: struct TouchSample { let slotID: Int; let playerID: Player.ID; let beganAt: TimeInterval; var endedAt: TimeInterval?; var wasCancelled: Bool }
interfaces.notes: Timestamps are UITouch.timestamp (hardware time), never Date() or SwiftUI dispatch time.

interfaces.name: VoidReason
interfaces.signature: enum VoidReason: String { case droppedTouch, backgrounded, manual }
interfaces.notes: backgrounded case closes the architecture-review gap: scenePhase changes mid-round void via GameEngine.handleScenePhaseChange, not just touchesCancelled. manual is a UI backstop since touchesCancelled isn't guaranteed to fire.

interfaces.name: ArenaVerdict
interfaces.signature: enum ArenaVerdict { case resolved(eliminated: [Player.ID], survivors: [Player.ID], reason: EliminationReason), voided(VoidReason) }
interfaces.notes: Return type of ArenaResolver; only value that crosses from the UIKit island back into GameEngine.

interfaces.name: ArenaResolver
interfaces.signature: enum ArenaResolver { static func resolve(samples: [TouchSample], triggerTime: TimeInterval) -> ArenaVerdict }
interfaces.notes: Pure, nonisolated, no UIKit/UIView dependency — unit-testable with synthetic timestamp arrays from any thread.

interfaces.name: ArmingDurationProviding
interfaces.signature: protocol ArmingDurationProviding { func nextDuration() -> TimeInterval }
interfaces.notes: Production impl RandomArmingDurationProvider(range: 1.5...3.0); tests inject a fixed-value stub. Animation driven by CADisplayLink inside ArenaUIView, never a SwiftUI @State timer.

interfaces.name: SettingsStore
interfaces.signature: @Observable @MainActor final class SettingsStore { var hapticsEnabled: Bool; var soundEnabled: Bool; var reduceMotionOverride: Bool; var accessibleModeDefault: Bool; func load(); func save() }
interfaces.notes: Backed by UserDefaults; accessibleModeDefault must never be gated by ProEntitlementState. load() falls back to defaults on decode issues.

interfaces.name: MatchRecord
interfaces.signature: struct MatchRecord: Identifiable, Codable { let id: UUID; let date: Date; let playerNames: [String]; let winnerName: String; let roundCount: Int }

interfaces.name: MatchHistoryStore
interfaces.signature: @MainActor final class MatchHistoryStore { func loadAll() -> [MatchRecord]; func append(_ record: MatchRecord); func clear() }
interfaces.notes: JSON file via FileManager, capped at last 50 records; corrupt file falls back to empty array, never throws past app launch.

interfaces.name: StoreManager
interfaces.signature: @Observable @MainActor final class StoreManager { private(set) var proEntitlement: ProEntitlementState; func purchasePro() async; func restorePurchases() async; func refreshEntitlements() async }
interfaces.notes: StoreKit 2, genuinely async/network-backed — the one real actor-hop boundary in the app. Entitlement cached via Transaction.currentEntitlements so Pro works offline at a party.

interfaces.name: ProEntitlementState
interfaces.signature: enum ProEntitlementState { case unknown, notPurchased, purchasing, purchased, error(PurchaseErrorReason) }
interfaces.notes: Reason must be distinguishable in the UI, not one generic error banner.

interfaces.name: HapticsEngine
interfaces.signature: @MainActor final class HapticsEngine { func prepare(); func fireTrigger(); func fireSurvival(); func fireFalseStart(); func fireSlowLiftElimination() }
interfaces.notes: prepare() must be called at arming-phase start, not trigger time, to avoid generator cold-start latency reading as trigger lag.

interfaces.name: MatchSnapshotStore
interfaces.signature: @MainActor final class MatchSnapshotStore { func save(_ match: Match); func loadSnapshot() -> Match?; func clearSnapshot() }
interfaces.notes: Written on scenePhase .background/.inactive; powers Home's 'Resume Game' affordance. Decode failure returns nil, never throws.

interfaces.name: AccessibleArenaView
interfaces.signature: struct AccessibleArenaView: View { let match: Match; let onVerdict: (ArenaVerdict) -> Void }
interfaces.notes: Sequential single-tap-per-player VoiceOver-friendly substitute for the simultaneous ArenaUIView; same ArenaVerdict/EliminationReason contract.

interfaces.name: MatchContainerView
interfaces.signature: struct MatchContainerView: View { var engine: GameEngine }
interfaces.notes: Single NavigationStack destination for the whole in-match experience; switches on engine.match.phase, no per-round push. Back-out is a single confirm-to-abandon alert.

interfaces.name: MatchStartError
interfaces.signature: enum MatchStartError: Error { case belowMinimumPlayers(minimum: Int), duplicateName(String) }
interfaces.notes: GameEngine.startMatch is the single source of truth for roster validity; PlayerSetupView surfaces the thrown error rather than duplicating validation.

interfaces.name: MatchPersistenceError
interfaces.signature: enum MatchPersistenceError: Error { case decodeFailed, writeFailed }
interfaces.notes: Thrown internally by MatchHistoryStore/MatchSnapshotStore only; both catch and degrade to empty/nil rather than propagate to UI or block app launch.

interfaces.name: PurchaseErrorReason
interfaces.signature: enum PurchaseErrorReason: String { case network, userCancelled, verificationFailed }
interfaces.notes: userCancelled dismisses silently; network shows a retry affordance; verificationFailed is the only case with a support path.
